title: dorycnium pentaphyllum proles decumbens
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum proles decumbens, also known as creeping restharrow or creeping dorycnium, is a sub-species of the perennial herb Dorycnium pentaphyllum, belonging to the legume family. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe, to treat various health issues. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of creeping restharrow include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms such as eczema, acne, and irritable bowel syndrome.
